description:
You are a network engineer who has just collected raw ping data and now need to build a concise JSON
    summary that management tools can ingest.

    1. A directory already exists at  
       /home/user/net_tests  

       Inside it there is a CSV file named ping_results.csv with the following exact contents (including the header row):

       host,status,latency_ms
       8.8.8.8,success,23
       1.1.1.1,success,19
       192.0.2.1,failed,
       example.com,success,45

       • Column meanings  
         host         – destination that was pinged  
         status       – literal string “success” or “failed”  
         latency_ms   – round-trip time in milliseconds; this column is empty when status == failed  

    2. Your tasks:

       a. Read ping_results.csv and create a JSON report called  
          /home/user/net_tests/ping_summary.json

       b. The JSON report must be a single, flat JSON object that exactly follows this schema
          (order of keys matters and will be tested verbatim):

          {
            "total_hosts": <integer>,
            "success_count": <integer>,
            "fail_count": <integer>,
            "avg_latency_ms": <float with one decimal place>,
            "failed_hosts": [<string>, ...]
          }

          • total_hosts      – number of rows in the CSV excluding the header  
          • success_count    – rows whose status == "success"  
          • fail_count       – rows whose status == "failed"  
          • avg_latency_ms   – arithmetic mean of latency_ms over successful rows only; must be rounded to one decimal place  
          • failed_hosts     – JSON array containing the host values whose status == "failed" (order preserved from CSV)

       c. Append a one-line entry to /home/user/net_tests/action.log recording that the summary was produced.
          The line must strictly follow this format (single space between parts):

          summary generated: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM

          Replace YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM with the current local date and time at execution down to minutes.
          Do NOT include seconds. An automated test will only check that
          (1) the line starts with “summary generated: ” and
          (2) the timestamp matches the prescribed layout.

    3. Constraints:

       • Use any CLI utilities available in a common Linux environment.  
       • The entire task can be completed with a simple pipeline of 2–3 commands.  
       • Do not remove or rename existing files; only create/append as specified.  
       • Ensure the JSON file is valid (parsable by jq).

    4. Verification the grader will perform:

       • Confirm that /home/user/net_tests/ping_summary.json exists.  
       • Parse it with jq and compare each key/value against the expected numbers.  
       • Open /home/user/net_tests/action.log, read the last line, and verify it matches the required prefix
         and timestamp format.

    Deliverables:

    • /home/user/net_tests/ping_summary.json containing the correctly calculated values in the
      exact key order.  
    • /home/user/net_tests/action.log with the appended “summary generated: …” line.
truth:
Expected JSON content for automated grading:

    {
      "total_hosts": 4,
      "success_count": 3,
      "fail_count": 1,
      "avg_latency_ms": 29.0,
      "failed_hosts": ["192.0.2.1"]
    }

    Full canonical string (no pretty-print, used by tests):

    {"total_hosts":4,"success_count":3,"fail_count":1,"avg_latency_ms":29.0,"failed_hosts":["192.0.2.1"]}

    Files that must exist before the agent starts:

    1. /home/user/net_tests/ping_results.csv  (exact contents given in task)
       Permissions: 644 (agent has read access)

    2. /home/user/net_tests/action.log
       • May be empty initially or absent; grader will not assume prior contents.
       • If the file does not exist before task execution, it is acceptable for the agent to create it.

    3. /home/user/net_tests directory itself with permissions 755.

    Automated checks:

    • jq -e '.total_hosts == 4 and .success_count == 3 and .fail_count == 1 and .avg_latency_ms == 29.0 and (.failed_hosts|length)==1 and .failed_hosts[0]=="192.0.2.1"' /home/user/net_tests/ping_summary.json
      must exit 0.

    • awk 'END{print}' /home/user/net_tests/action.log
      must match regex: ^summary generated: [0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}$
name: task_000000_07f0fa10
